title: Melusine
summary: The repository artifact is an exact pinned copy of a University of Michigan electronic text made in 1997 from Kraus Reprint's 1981 reissue of Alexander Karley Donald's 1895 EETS edition. Donald transcribed the complete anonymous Middle English prose Melusine from British Library Royal MS 18 B II, while consulting the 1854 Brunet French edition and, at selected points, British Library Harley MS 4418. The U-M encoding represents Donald's complete romance on printed pp. 1-371 and six selected notes, but omits the title page, contents, most notes, glossary, and indexes. Although Donald labeled the volume Part I, the romance itself is complete; the projected later part concerned editorial apparatus rather than a continuation of the narrative. Royal MS 18 B II has no public whole-manuscript facsimile located as of 2026-07-15, but physical access and reproduction can be requested from the British Library. A complete 532-canvas IIIF bundle of the supporting French Harley MS 4418, complete scans of Donald 1895 and Brunet 1854, a complete but imperfect copy of Adam Steinschaber's 1478 French print, and relevant scholarship are cached privately. Scholarship supports a family-level derivation of the English translation from Steinschaber's 1478 text or a very close incunable; it does not identify the Mazarine copy as the translator's physical exemplar.
review_notes: The cataloged work is the anonymous Middle English prose translation and adaptation, not Jean d'Arras's French work, Royal MS 18 B II, Donald's edition, or either digital encoding.
Donald prints the complete romance on pp. 1-371. Part I describes an incomplete publication plan, not an incomplete narrative text.
Royal MS 18 B II is the base witness. Harley MS 4418 and Brunet 1854 are supporting French comparison sources, not ancestors in the English manuscript's physical-copy chain.

The current MEDictionary mirror XML is a distinct representation: it has an ETS/EEBO wrapper, generic ID CME00000, current U-M rights wording, CHOICE structures, and different markup from the pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS artifact.
The U-M header says that only correction notes were retained, but direct inspection finds six notes of several kinds; this observed boundary is recorded instead of repeating the narrower claim.
The public Mazarine Inc. 1284 digitization is a complete surrogate of that repository object, but the underlying 1478 copy is itself imperfect.
The Harley MS 4418 ZIP contains all 532 IIIF canvases, including bindings, flyleaves, blanks, and spine. The French work occupies canvases 16-519, labeled f. 1r-f. 251v.
The CME repository Unlicense is limited to contributor-controlled material and does not override U-M, British Library, Internet Archive, Institut de France, or scholarly-publication terms.

entities.label: General Corpus pinned repository copy of Melusine.xml
entities.description: The 725,663-byte DLPSTEXTCLASS XML artifact used by this repository.

entities.label: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative Melusine encoding
entities.description: The 1997 electronic text encoded from the 1981 Kraus reprint of Donald's edition.

bibliographic.citation: Melusine, electronic text Melusine (Ann Arbor: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative, 1997), encoded from the 1981 Kraus reprint of A. K. Donald's 1895 EETS edition.
bibliographic.title: Melusine. Part I
bibliographic.publisher: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative

entities.label: Current MEDictionary-hosted Melusine XML representation
entities.description: A publicly downloadable XML representation with an ETS/EEBO wrapper and current U-M rights statement; its bytes and markup are not identical to the pinned repository artifact.
notes: The wrapper's IDG ID is CME00000 rather than the work-specific UMDL identifier.
The body has 63 DIV1 elements, 371 PB elements, 435 milestones, and six body notes; it preserves 23 CHOICE/CORR/SIC structures that the pinned transformation largely flattens.

entities.label: Kraus Reprint of Donald's Melusine edition (1981)
entities.description: The immediate print source named by the U-M header: a Millwood, New York reissue of EETS Extra Series 68.

bibliographic.title: Melusine
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 68

entities.label: A. K. Donald's EETS edition of Melusine (1895)
entities.description: The first complete print edition of the Royal MS 18 B II Middle English romance: text pp. 1-371, notes and illustrations pp. 373-388, glossary pp. 389-400, and indexes pp. 401-408.

bibliographic.citation: Jean d'Arras, Melusine, compiled 1382-1394 A.D., Englisht about 1500, ed. A. K. Donald, Part I: Text, Notes, and Glossary, EETS Extra Series 68 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1895).
bibliographic.title: Melusine
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 68

notes: Part I marks Donald's publication package. The complete romance and colophon are printed before the apparatus.
Only this part was published; the planned continuation concerned fuller critical apparatus and introduction.

entities.label: Internet Archive facsimile of Donald's 1895 edition
entities.description: A complete 430-page digital surrogate with PDF, OCR, page-number metadata, scan data, and item metadata.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images, OCR text, and scan metadata

entities.label: EETS prospectus for the projected second part of Melusine (1898)
entities.description: A contemporary EETS publication prospectus identifying the projected second part as an introduction with ten facsimiles of foreign black-letter woodblocks, a glossary, and related apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Early English Text Society publication prospectus, preliminary advertisement bound with the 1898 reprint of The Romance of the Cheuelere Assigne, PDF p. 3.
bibliographic.title: EETS publication prospectus

entities.label: Anonymous Middle English prose Melusine textual layer
entities.description: The intellectual Middle English translation and adaptation shared by its continuous Royal manuscript witness and the textually affiliated early print fragments. This abstract layer separates the translated work from any one physical carrier.

date_statements.label: translation and adaptation
date_statements.value: after August 1478; before or during the making of Royal MS 18 B II

notes: Royal MS 18 B II is the earliest and most complete continuous carrier, but it is not conflated with the intellectual translation itself.
The print-fragment relationship remains tentative because direct abridgment and descent from a separate common exemplar are competing hypotheses.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 B II
entities.description: The earliest and most complete continuous witness of the anonymous Middle English prose Melusine and Donald's base manuscript.

date_statements.label: catalog date
date_statements.notes: The British Library supplies the broad range. Watermark comparison supports an 1480s context but does not prove a narrower copying year.

physical_description.format: Single-column codex, 37-38 lines per page, Gothic cursive
physical_description.dimensions: 285 x 200 mm; written space approximately 200 x 130 mm
notes: The work occupies ff. 1r-219v: prologue ff. 1r-3v, translator's estates list at f. 3v, and romance ff. 3v-219v.
The narrative proper ends at f. 217v; concluding paragraphs abridged from French precede the colophon on f. 219v.

entities.label: Four partial Royal MS 18 B II photographs embedded in Colquitt 2023
entities.description: Four manuscript photographs illustrating watermarks and scribal hands at ff. 1r, 33r, 32r, and 217r; not a whole-manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: Four photographs embedded in a DOCX article manuscript

entities.label: Olivia Colquitt's 2023 study of Royal MS 18 B II
entities.description: A codicological note reassessing the manuscript's date, script, watermarks, and relationship to the 1478 Steinschaber print family.

bibliographic.citation: Olivia Colquitt, study of British Library Royal MS 18 B II, Notes and Queries 70 (2023): 7-11, doi:10.1093/notesj/gjac130.

entities.label: Lydia Zeldenrust's study of the English Melusine fragments (2017)
entities.description: A scholarly study of fragmentary early printed English Melusine witnesses and their relation to Royal MS 18 B II and the French print family.

bibliographic.citation: Lydia Zeldenrust, The Fragments of a Middle English Melusyne Edition: Some Further Clues, Journal of the Early Book Society 20 (2017): 251-264.
bibliographic.container_title: Journal of the Early Book Society

entities.label: Six English Melusine print fragments in Bodleian Vet. A1 d.18
entities.description: Six surviving leaves from an early English printed Melusine, probably associated with Wynkyn de Worde and often dated cautiously to around 1510.
notes: The fragments' textual affiliation with Royal MS 18 B II is strong, but whether they abridge the manuscript version or descend separately from a common exemplar remains unresolved.

entities.label: Steinschaber 1478 Melusine textual family
entities.description: The French prose print family represented by Adam Steinschaber's Geneva edition of August 1478. Scholarship supports this edition or a very close incunable as the English translator's source at family level.
notes: This entity prevents a false claim that any surviving physical copy, including Mazarine Inc. 1284, was the translator's exemplar.

entities.label: Adam Steinschaber's Geneva Melusine edition (1478)
entities.description: The French prose incunable printed at Geneva in August 1478 and the best-supported source-edition family for the Middle English translation.

bibliographic.citation: Jean d'Arras, Mélusine (Geneva: Adam Steinschaber, August 1478).
bibliographic.title: Mélusine

entities.label: Bibliothèque Mazarine Inc. 1284
entities.description: An imperfect surviving copy of Steinschaber's 1478 Melusine edition, cataloged as 160 leaves and digitized in 401 views.

notes: The copy is incomplete; completeness of the digital surrogate is relative to this physical object.

entities.label: Institut de France digital facsimile of Mazarine Inc. 1284
entities.description: A complete 401-view digital surrogate of the imperfect Mazarine copy, publicly available as page images, PDF, and IIIF.

entities.label: Charles Brunet's Melusine edition (1854)
entities.description: A French edition described as conforming to the 1478 edition, reviewed and corrected, and consulted by Donald.

bibliographic.citation: Jean d'Arras, Melusine: nouvelle édition conforme à celle de 1478, revue et corrigée, with preface by Charles Brunet (Paris: P. Jannet, 1854).
bibliographic.title: Melusine

entities.label: Internet Archive facsimile of Brunet's 1854 Melusine
entities.description: A complete digital surrogate of the 1854 printed edition.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page images and item metadata

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 4418
entities.description: An imperfect illustrated French prose Melusine manuscript from northern France, consulted by Donald as a supporting comparison source.

notes: Imperfect prologue ff. 1r-1v, imperfect table ff. 2r-7v, and imperfect romance ff. 8r-251v; incomplete at both ends.

entities.label: British Library IIIF facsimile of Harley MS 4418
entities.description: A public 532-canvas whole-object facsimile covering bindings, spine, flyleaves, blanks, and all 251 folios.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and JPEG page images

rights.component: Contributor-controlled repository material in the pinned CME repository
rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide, to the extent the contributor controls the material
rights.basis: The pinned CME repository contains an Unlicense dedication and license grant.
notes: This does not override rights or terms inherited from U-M or other upstream providers.

rights.component: Imported U-M Melusine XML content
rights.basis: The current U-M item says Public Domain / No Copyright-United States, while the imported 1997 header retains a restrictive redistribution notice.
notes: U-M clarification is prudent before redistributing the imported XML.

rights.component: The digital text and materials presented on the current U-M item
rights.basis: The current U-M item labels the material Public Domain and uses No Copyright-United States.
notes: No Copyright-United States is jurisdiction-specific rather than a worldwide license.

rights.component: The 1997 availability notice embedded in the encoding
rights.basis: The embedded notice permits individuals to make copies for personal use, research, and teaching when the notice is retained; it separately permits Internet editions, including for-profit works, to link to the text, while forbidding redistribution by commercial and nonprofit content providers.

rights.component: The current downloadable XML representation
rights.basis: The file carries current U-M public-domain wording, but MEDictionary's independent redistribution terms for the hosted representation were not established.

rights.component: The anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English translation and adaptation
rights.basis: The anonymous work was created and copied in the fifteenth century.
notes: This does not determine rights in modern editions, digital encodings, or manuscript photographs.

rights.component: Any reprint-specific typography, production matter, or additions in the 1981 Kraus volume
rights.jurisdiction: United States and other applicable jurisdictions
rights.basis: The immediate source was published in 1981, but no physical or digital copy was inspected to determine whether it is purely photographic or contains separately protectable additions.
notes: The underlying 1895 edition and medieval text are separately recorded as public domain in the United States.

rights.component: The 1898 prospectus and Wikimedia Commons scan
rights.basis: The prospectus dates to 1898, and Wikimedia Commons marks the complete file Public domain.

rights.component: Donald's 1895 editorial text and apparatus
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1895.

rights.component: Internet Archive digital surrogate and derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying 1895 edition is public domain in the United States; no separate exclusive claim was located for the scan layer.

rights.component: The medieval manuscript text as an underlying historical work
rights.basis: The manuscript and anonymous Middle English work date to the fifteenth century.
notes: This does not determine rights or contractual terms in newly ordered images.

rights.component: New digital images ordered from the British Library
rights.basis: No image set or license has yet been supplied; reuse must be determined from the quotation, order terms, and written licensing response.

rights.component: Modern scholarly article text
rights.basis: The 2023 article remains modern copyrighted scholarship despite open repository access.

rights.component: Four British Library manuscript photographs embedded in the article
rights.basis: The figures credit the British Library Board, and the author records permission for their publication.
notes: Permission for Colquitt's article does not automatically license redistribution in this repository.

rights.component: Modern scholarly article text
rights.basis: White Rose warns that copyright and further copying restrictions may apply to deposited material.

rights.component: The 1478 French printed text
rights.basis: The edition was printed in 1478.

rights.component: Institut de France page images and metadata for Mazarine Inc. 1284
rights.basis: The item record describes the digitized item as public domain; Institut terms condition free reuse on noncommercial CC BY-NC-ND 3.0 France terms and require prior authorization and a fee for commercial reuse.
notes: Commercial print-on-demand or repository redistribution should be cleared with bibnum@institutdefrance.fr rather than inferred from the public-domain item label.

rights.component: The 1854 French edition

rights.basis: The underlying 1854 edition is public domain in the United States; no separate exclusive claim was located for the scan layer.

rights.component: The medieval French manuscript text
rights.basis: The manuscript dates to c. 1440-c. 1450.

rights.component: British Library IIIF page images

rights.component: British Library IIIF page images

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete Middle English text in Royal MS 18 B II, with French comparison materials
editorial_practices.representation: A continuous edited transcription on pp. 1-371 with notes and glossary
editorial_practices.description: Donald transcribed Royal MS 18 B II as the base text and used Brunet 1854 and Harley MS 4418 for selected French comparison and apparatus.
editorial_practices.scope: The complete romance and Donald's supporting apparatus

editorial_practices.source_feature: The complete Donald/Kraus publication package, including title page, contents, text, notes, glossary, and indexes
editorial_practices.representation: The romance on pp. 1-371 and six selected notes
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding retains the complete romance but omits the title page, contents, most notes, glossary, and indexes.
notes: Direct inspection found 63 DIV1 units, 371 PB and EPB page markers, 435 milestones, and six NOTE1 elements in the body.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Print abbreviations represented by C-slash and S-slash forms
editorial_practices.description: The header records replacement of C-slash with one hundred on printed pp. 198 and 292 and S-slash with soublz on p. 356.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The U-M electronic text and correction markup
editorial_practices.representation: ETS/EEBO wrapper, HI markup, PB REF attributes, and 23 explicit CHOICE/CORR/SIC structures
editorial_practices.description: The current XML and pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS file preserve closely related textual content but are not byte-identical or structurally interchangeable; comparison found 25 differing normalized body blocks.
editorial_practices.scope: The complete current and pinned XML representations

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative, Melusine.xml, General Corpus pinned CME submodule copy.
evidence.locator: Header, source description, availability, revision history, and full body structure
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1997 U-M encoding, 1981 Kraus reprint, Donald's 1895 EETS edition, and Royal MS 18 B II; records legacy rights and encoding practices. Direct inspection establishes the artifact hash, complete-romance boundary, six retained notes, and omitted paratext.
notes: The file is 725,663 bytes, Git blob f3fdd5dea7c7be09c4cd49768323bea025a1e45c at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
Observed body structure: 63 DIV1, 64 HEAD, 266 P, 371 PB, 371 EPB, 435 MILESTONE, and six NOTE1 elements.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository, LICENSE and pinned Git state.
evidence.summary: Establishes the exact CME commit used by General Corpus and its Unlicense dedication; the license applies only to material controlled by the contributor.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Melusine.
evidence.locator: Item metadata, navigation, and rights statement
evidence.summary: Provides the current public digital text, editor and source metadata, and labels the item Public Domain with No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: MEDictionary, Melusine.xml, current downloadable XML representation.
evidence.locator: ETS/EEBO wrapper, availability, IDG, and body markup
evidence.summary: Supplies a 740,271-byte current XML representation with current U-M public-domain wording, generic ID CME00000, 63 DIV1 elements, 371 PB elements, 435 milestones, six body notes, and 23 explicit CHOICE structures.

evidence.citation: Direct structural and normalized-text comparison of the pinned and current Melusine XML representations.
evidence.locator: Document roots, element counts, correction structures, and normalized DIV1 body blocks
evidence.summary: The current ETS file and pinned DLPSTEXTCLASS artifact represent the same U-M edition selection but differ in wrapper, identifiers, rights text, HI/HI1 and NOTE/NOTE1 vocabulary, EPB handling, CHOICE flattening, and 25 normalized body blocks. They are modeled as distinct representations rather than byte-identical copies.

evidence.citation: Jean d'Arras, Melusine, ed. A. K. Donald, EETS Extra Series 68 (London, 1895), Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: Title page; text pp. 1-371; notes pp. 373-388; glossary pp. 389-400; indexes pp. 401-408
evidence.summary: Identifies Royal MS 18 B II as Donald's source and establishes the full publication boundary. The romance and colophon are complete on pp. 1-371 even though the title calls the volume Part I. Donald's apparatus cites Brunet and Harley MS 4418.
notes: The PDF is 24,063,020 bytes and 430 pages; PDF pages 17-387 correspond to printed pp. 1-371.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for melusine00jeanuoft.
evidence.summary: Supplies item-level provenance, file inventory, access URLs, and public-domain-era publication metadata for the complete Donald scan.

evidence.citation: The Online Books Page, entry for A. K. Donald's Melusine.
evidence.summary: Records that Part I was the only part published and provides stable links to the public scan; combined with the volume contents, this distinguishes the complete romance from the incomplete publication plan.

evidence.citation: Early English Text Society publication prospectus, preliminary advertisement bound with the 1898 reprint of The Romance of the Cheuelere Assigne, PDF p. 3.
evidence.locator: PDF page 3, EETS plans for Extra-Series texts
evidence.summary: The contemporary notice describes the projected second part of the prose Romance of Melusine as an introduction with ten facsimiles of woodblocks from foreign black-letter editions, a glossary, and related apparatus. It therefore confirms that Part II was not intended as a continuation of the romance narrative. Wikimedia Commons marks the complete 82-page file Public domain.
notes: The cached PDF is 4,736,174 bytes and preserves the entire source publication, not only the prospectus page.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 18 B II, Romance of Melusine.
evidence.locator: Summary, scope and content, physical description, dates, and Digitised Content
evidence.summary: Describes the paper codex, broad date c. 1470-c. 1498, prologue ff. 1r-3v, estates list at f. 3v, romance ff. 3v-219v, concluding paragraphs, colophon, physical dimensions, and holding. The Digitised Content field is blank.

evidence.citation: Dated exact-shelfmark search for a complete public facsimile of British Library Royal MS 18 B II.
evidence.summary: No complete public facsimile was located on 2026-07-15: the official record has no Digitised Content, exact BL IIIF/Digirati discovery produced no usable object, and Arlima labels the manuscript not digitized.
notes: This is a dated negative finding, not proof that no unindexed internal surrogate exists.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services and Archives and Manuscripts access forms.
evidence.summary: Provides the routes for physical-manuscript access and requesting a fee-based digital reproduction; publication and reuse questions can be directed to British Library licensing.

evidence.citation: British Library, Terms of Use.
evidence.summary: Provides provider terms relevant to British Library catalog records, requested reproductions, and digital images.

evidence.citation: Olivia Colquitt, study of Royal MS 18 B II, Notes and Queries 70 (2023): 7-11.
evidence.summary: Argues that Royal is the earliest and most complete continuous English witness, finds no concrete basis for the older c. 1500 date, identifies watermark parallels suggesting an 1480s context, and supports Steinschaber 1478 or a close incunable as source. Four partial BL photographs show ff. 1r, 33r, 32r, and 217r.

evidence.citation: Lydia Zeldenrust, The Fragments of a Middle English Melusyne Edition: Some Further Clues, Journal of the Early Book Society 20 (2017): 251-264.
evidence.summary: Documents six Bodleian printed fragments, STC 14648 / USTC 501139, and their strong textual affiliation with Royal; preserves uncertainty between abridgment and a separate common exemplar and supports the Steinschaber print family as the English tradition's French source.

evidence.citation: Bibliothèque numérique de l'Institut de France, Melusine, Mazarine Inc. 1284.
evidence.locator: Bibliographic description, extent, rights, and attached PDF
evidence.summary: Identifies the Geneva 1478 Adam Steinschaber edition, Mazarine shelfmark Inc. 1284, 160-leaf folio, 401-view integral digitization, and public-domain item status.

evidence.citation: Institut de France IIIF Presentation manifest for Mazarine Inc. 1284.
evidence.summary: The manifest enumerates 401 canvases corresponding to the complete public digital surrogate.

evidence.citation: Mélusine Romance in Medieval Europe, appendix: Manuscripts and Printed Editions of the Various Mélusine Versions up to c. 1600.
evidence.summary: Provides a current scholarly census of early Melusine witnesses and editions and identifies the Mazarine 1478 copy as incomplete.

evidence.citation: Bibliothèque numérique de l'Institut de France, Mentions légales.
evidence.summary: States noncommercial reuse terms for public-domain images and metadata under CC BY-NC-ND 3.0 France and requires prior authorization and a fee for commercial reuse.

evidence.citation: Jean d'Arras, Melusine, ed. Charles Brunet (Paris: P. Jannet, 1854), Internet Archive scan.
evidence.summary: The title describes the edition as conforming to the 1478 edition, reviewed and corrected, with a Brunet preface. Donald cites and compares this edition.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for melusine01jeangoog.
evidence.summary: Supplies item-level metadata and file access for the complete Brunet scan.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 4418, Jean d'Arras, Roman de Mélusine.
evidence.locator: Summary, dates, contents, physical description, and digitized-content link
evidence.summary: Describes the northern French parchment manuscript, c. 1440-c. 1450, 251 folios, imperfect prologue and table, imperfect romance ff. 8r-251v, and 17 miniatures.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation manifest for Harley MS 4418.
evidence.summary: Enumerates the complete manuscript object from front through spine. Canvas 16 is f. 1r and canvas 519 is f. 251v. The manifest states that the images are public domain in most countries other than the UK.

evidence.citation: Integrity audit of the cached whole-manuscript IIIF bundle for Harley MS 4418.
evidence.summary: ZIP integrity passes; manifest, inventory, and image count all equal 532. Inventory checks map canvas 16 to f. 1r, canvas 519 to f. 251v, and canvas 532 to the spine, confirming whole-object coverage and exact work locators.
notes: The ZIP is 474,477,696 bytes and contains per-image SHA-256 and byte counts in inventory.json.

open_questions.question: Does the British Library hold an unindexed whole-manuscript digital surrogate of Royal MS 18 B II, and what would a new complete color reproduction cost and permit?
next_steps: Await the British Library response to the submitted enquiry.
If ordering, specify covers, boards, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves and blanks, and all leaves, with the work mapped to ff. 1r-219v.
Obtain written reuse terms covering repository reference access and any future print-on-demand context before publishing images.

open_questions.question: Which physical French incunable, if any surviving copy, was used by the anonymous Middle English translator?
next_steps: Retain the family-level Steinschaber 1478 relation unless new stemmatic or codicological evidence identifies a physical exemplar.
Do not substitute the publicly digitized Mazarine copy for an unidentified source copy.

open_questions.question: Do the early English printed fragments abridge the Royal MS 18 B II text directly, or do both descend from a separate common exemplar?
next_steps: Preserve both hypotheses and avoid a direct copied-from edge.
Review later stemmatic work and any newly identified fragment leaves.

open_questions.question: How does U-M apply its current Public Domain / No Copyright-United States statement to redistribution in light of the restrictive availability notice embedded in the 1997 encoding?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections which statement governs a new public redistribution of the encoding.
Preserve attribution and the embedded notice until U-M clarifies the conflict.

open_questions.question: What permission or current provider terms apply to publishing Harley MS 4418 IIIF images in the United Kingdom?
next_steps: Contact licensing@bl.uk with the IIIF manifest URI and intended repository or print-on-demand use.
Retain the manifest's exact non-UK public-domain qualification until written clarification is received.

open_questions.question: Was the 1981 Kraus volume a photographic reprint or a reset edition, and did it introduce any content changes relative to 1895?
next_steps: Inspect a physical or digitized 1981 copy page-for-page against the 1895 scan.
Until then, retain version_of rather than same_as.
